seo.title: Idaho Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Idaho — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license idaho, dental assistant certification idaho, RDA idaho, DANB CDA idaho, dental assistant radiography idaho

board.name: Idaho State Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address:
Idaho State Board of Dentistry
11341 W. Chinden Blvd.
Building #4
Boise, ID 83714

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Idaho.

steps.title: Understand Scope of Practice
steps.description: Dental Assistants in Idaho perform duties under the direct supervision of a licensed dentist, as outlined by Idaho State Board of Dentistry rules and regulations.

steps.title: Review Idaho State Board of Dentistry Rules
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with the Idaho Rules and Regulations governing the practice of dentistry and the delegation of duties to dental assistants, specifically IDAPA 24.02.01, which can be found on the Board's website.

steps.title: Ensure Competency and Training
steps.description: Dentists are responsible for ensuring that their dental assistants are adequately trained and competent to perform any delegated tasks, as there are no state-mandated training or examination requirements for general duties.

steps.title: No State-Level Registration or Licensure
steps.description: There is no formal application process, state registration, or licensure required for dental assistants by the Idaho State Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Idaho does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Idaho require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Idaho does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Idaho?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Idaho.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Idaho?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Idaho?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Idaho. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Idaho does not license general dental assistants. Nitrous oxide permit available.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Idaho
hero.dek: Dental Assistants are not licensed by the Idaho State Board of Dentistry. They are permitted to perform duties according to their training under the direct supervision of a licensed dentist.

official_links.registration_steps: Not applicable, as Idaho does not license Dental Assistants. The linked page provides regulatory information.
